# `lr-code-block-core`

- **Import** `import '@aceshooting/lyra-ui/components/conversation/code-block/code-block-core.js';` (registers the tag; side-effect import)
- **Class** `LyraCodeBlockCore`, also available unregistered from `@aceshooting/lyra-ui/components/conversation/code-block/code-block-core.class.js`
- **Family** `components/conversation/` ‚Äî see `llms/index.md` for its siblings
- **Optional peers** `shiki` ‚Äî see `llms/peers.md`
- **Library-wide behavior** (events, form association, `locale`/`strings`, tokens, TS types): `llms/shared.md`

---

## `lr-code-block-core`

A build-lean sibling of `<lr-code-block>` above, for a consumer whose `languages` map already
covers every language it will ever render. Where `<lr-code-block>` unconditionally calls
`loadShikiHighlighter()` ‚Äî the default ~200-language dynamic-import table loader, whose bundled
lookup table a bundler can't statically narrow away even when a consumer never actually uses it ‚Äî
this component's own module never imports or calls that function at all. It only ever calls
`loadShikiHighlighterCore(languages)` (shiki's "fine-grained bundle" recipe: `createHighlighterCore()`
plus an explicit oniguruma engine, seeded with *only* the grammars in `languages`), so a consumer
importing this entry point instead of `code-block.js` gets a build genuinely free of shiki's full
language table.

A `language` value absent from `languages` always renders the plain `<pre><code>` fallback ‚Äî there is
no default/full-table highlighter here to fall back to, unlike `<lr-code-block>`'s dynamic-import
path for an unmapped language. That fallback is the *default* rendering path, not a degraded one,
same as `<lr-code-block>`'s own plain-text fallback. Everything else ‚Äî `code`/`language`/
`filename`/`copyable`/`collapsible`/`collapsed`/`maxHeight`, the copy button, the collapse header
toggle, the loading-skeleton behavior while the fine-grained highlighter resolves ‚Äî matches
`<lr-code-block>` exactly, including its CSS parts, themeable custom properties, and stylesheet
(this component reuses `code-block.styles.ts` directly).

**Properties:**
- `code: string = ''` ‚Äî the raw source text.
- `language: string = ''` ‚Äî a shiki-recognized language id or alias; when unset, or when it isn't a
  key in `languages`, the code renders as plain unhighlighted text ‚Äî this component has no
  default/full-table highlighter to fall back to.
- `filename: string = ''` ‚Äî shown in the header, when set.
- `accessibleLabel: string = ''` (attribute `aria-label`) ‚Äî names the internal focusable code-body
  region; otherwise a localized filename/language description is generated.
- `collapsible: boolean = false` (reflected) ‚Äî shows the collapse/expand chevron button.
- `collapsed: boolean = false` (reflected) ‚Äî only has a visible effect while `collapsible` is also
  true.
- `copyable: boolean = true` (reflected) ‚Äî shows the copy-to-clipboard button.
- `maxHeight: string = ''` (attribute `max-height`) ‚Äî a CSS length (e.g. `"20rem"`); once set, the
  code scrolls internally past this height instead of growing the page.
- `lineNumbers: boolean = false` (attribute `line-numbers`, reflected) ‚Äî displays one-based line
  numbers for highlighted and plain output.
- `highlightLines: string = ''` (attribute `highlight-lines`) ‚Äî comma-separated 1-based inclusive
  line ranges (e.g. `"3-5,7"`) to visually emphasize. Declarative sugar over `highlights` ‚Äî merges
  with, and renders identically to, any `line-range` entries there.
- `interactiveLines: boolean = false` (attribute `interactive-lines`) ‚Äî turns the
  (`lineNumbers`-gated) gutter into a roving-tabindex group of buttons emitting `lr-line-click`.
  Has no effect while `lineNumbers` is unset.
- `highlights: LyraHighlight[] = []` (attribute: false) ‚Äî host-supplied highlights to paint over the
  code. Only `line-range` anchors are meaningful here ‚Äî every other `LyraAnchor` kind is ignored.
- `activeHighlightId: string | null = null` (attribute `active-highlight-id`) ‚Äî the `highlights`
  entry, if any, currently treated as active (`data-active` on its lines).
- `anchorKinds: LyraAnchor['kind'][]` ‚Äî readonly `['line-range']`, for the shared anchor-target
  contract, identical to `<lr-code-block>`.
- `languages: Record<string, ShikiLanguageInput> = {}` (attribute: false) ‚Äî grammar definitions this
  instance can highlight, e.g. `{ json: jsonGrammar }` (import from `shiki/langs/<name>.mjs`). Empty
  (the default) never highlights at all ‚Äî every `language` renders the plain-text fallback.

**Methods:** `scrollToAnchor(target)` ‚Äî resolves a `line-range` anchor (or a `highlights` id string
resolving to one) by scrolling its start line into view within `[part="body"]`; resolves `false`
when the anchor isn't a `line-range`, the id isn't found, or the start line is out of bounds.
Identical behavior to `<lr-code-block>`'s own method.

**Events:** `lr-copy` (`detail: { text: string }` ‚Äî always the raw `code` value, never the
highlighted HTML, and always fires regardless of whether the actual OS clipboard write succeeded),
`lr-toggle` (`detail: { collapsed: boolean }` ‚Äî fired when the built-in collapse/expand header
button is activated), `lr-line-click` (`detail: { line: number }` ‚Äî a gutter line number was
activated while `interactiveLines` is set), `lr-highlight-activate` (`detail: { id: string }` ‚Äî
declared for parity with this library's other anchor-target viewers; not currently emitted by this
component), `lr-text-select` (`detail: { text, anchor, rects }` ‚Äî a text selection inside the code
body ended; `anchor` is a `line-range` anchor covering the selected lines).

**Slots:** none.

**CSS parts:** `base`, `header`, `filename`, `language`, `copy-button`, `toggle`, `body`, `pre`,
`code`, `line-highlight`, `line-button` ‚Äî identical set to `<lr-code-block>`.

**Themeable custom properties:** identical to `<lr-code-block>` ‚Äî `--lr-code-block-max-height`,
`--lr-code-block-font`, `--lr-code-block-tab-size` (default `2`, applied to `[part='pre']`),
`--lr-code-block-active-line-outline-color` (default `var(--lr-color-brand)`), plus the same shared
tokens. Both of the last two are inline `var()` fallbacks at the point of use rather than `:host`
declarations, so a page-, container-, or theme-level value reaches them; see `<lr-code-block>` above
for the full rationale, including why `<lr-markdown>`/`<lr-markdown-core>` must declare the tab-size
fallback separately.

**Optional peer deps:** `shiki` (specifically its `shiki/core`, `shiki/engine/oniguruma`,
`shiki/wasm`, and `shiki/themes/github-{light,dark}.mjs` subpaths ‚Äî never `shiki`'s main entry point,
which is what carries the ~200-language table this component exists to avoid). Building the
fine-grained highlighter is cached per `languages` object identity (a `WeakMap`), so passing the same
module-level `languages` constant on every render builds it only once.

```html
<script type="module">
  import jsonGrammar from 'shiki/langs/json.mjs';
</script>
<lr-code-block-core
  language="json"
  .languages=${{ json: jsonGrammar }}
  .code=${'{"ok": true}'}
></lr-code-block-core>
```

**Known gotchas:**
- there is no default highlighter and no dynamic-import fallback table ‚Äî a `language` you haven't
  added to `languages` will never highlight, no matter how common that language is elsewhere. Reach
  for `<lr-code-block>` instead if you need to support an open-ended set of languages without
  pre-declaring each one.
- `languages` is keyed by object identity for caching purposes ‚Äî pass a stable, module-level constant
  (not a fresh object literal per render), or every render rebuilds its own fine-grained highlighter.

---
